title: [Arcanine] Battle Guide
summary: A [Fire]-type physical attacker that uses [intimidate] on entry and patches its Speed tier with [extreme-speed] priority.
strengths: [extreme-speed] is one of the best priority moves in the game
weaknesses: Speed 95 loses to most upper-tier attackers

recommendedMoves.reason: Elite priority move to patch the Speed problem
Healing utility to offset re[coil] damage

bodyMarkdown:
## Overview
[Arcanine] is a [Fire]-type physical attacker that balances [intimidate] utility, [extreme-speed] priority, and reliable 110 Attack damage. 90/80/80 bulk with Speed 95 makes it an attacker that can also [pivot] through tight matchups, and [morning-sun] keeps it alive across long exchanges. Its Champions role blends offensive pressure with defensive support.

## Strengths
[intimidate] softens incoming physical attackers every time [Arcanine] switches in, rai[sing] its effective bulk without investment. [extreme-speed] is one of the best priority moves in the metagame — it guarantees KOs against weakened cleaners regardless of Speed stat. [flare-blitz] combines 110 Attack, STAB, and [jolly] nature boost to hit for enormous damage on any neutral target. [morning-sun] provides long-game healing that re[coil]-heavy sets otherwise cannot afford.

## Weaknesses & Answers
[Rock], [Water], and [Ground] 2x weaknesses are common cove[rage], and [stealth-rock] damage limits [Arcanine] reentry frequency. Speed 95 loses to the upper-tier 110+ attackers, so it cannot race dedicated sweepers without priority. [flare-blitz] re[coil] accumulates across the match, and the team needs hazard removal to offset the constant chip.

## Recommended Moveset
[flare-blitz] is the locked main STAB because the damage ceiling is what makes [Arcanine] threatening. [extreme-speed] is the Speed patch and cleaner move, guaranteeing KOs against faster weakened threats. [close-combat] is the [Fighting] cove[rage] for [Rock], [Normal], and [Steel] walls. [morning-sun] is the last slot to offset [flare-blitz] re[coil]. A [wild-charge] variant adds [Water] cove[rage] at the cost of the healing slot.

## Item · Ability · Tera
[charcoal] is the default because its 20% boost to [Fire] damage directly scales [flare-blitz] and [extreme-speed] is type-neutral. [leftovers] enables a long-game attrition plan and [sitrus-berry] offers a one-shot [recover]y for critical turns. Ability is [intimidate] — [flash]-[fire] and justified are weaker picks. Tera [Fire] maxes [flare-blitz] damage while Tera [Fighting] flips the [Rock] weakness and boosts [close-combat].

## Teammates & Synergy
[Arcanine] needs hazard removal ([defog] or [rapid-spin]) to manage [flare-blitz] re[coil] and [stealth-rock] chip. Pair it with [Grass] or [Ground] partners to cover [Water] and [Rock] weaknesses, and use [volt-switch] or [u-turn] [pivot]s to bring it in cleanly. A cleaner behind it capitalises on the chip [extreme-speed] leaves behind.

## Play Patterns
Switch in on a predicted physical attack to trigger [intimidate], then [flare-blitz] the weakened target. Use [extreme-speed] to finish faster cleaners, and heal with [morning-sun] once HP drops below 60%. Tera on any telegraphed [Rock] or [Water] move.
